label: 245_retirement_retirement savings_automatic ira_ira
description: Proposals to automatically enroll workers in IRAs aim to increase retirement savings among those without access to employer-sponsored plans. Experts highlight the significant gap in retirement security faced by many Americans, particularly small business employees, and advocate for policies that can bridge this gap through automatic enrollment mechanisms.

earliest_articles.title: Economic Growth and Institutional Innovation: Outlines of a Reform Agenda
earliest_articles.excerpt: 401(k) retirement plans, even a modest inconvenience will deter many of them from participating. But if you reverse the procedure— automatically enrolling them unless they affirmatively opt out—you can boost participation. To achieve an adequate rate of private saving, we may need to go even further. One option is a…

earliest_articles.title: New Legislation Helps Build Retirement Security
earliest_articles.excerpt: Both Senator Jeff Bingaman (D-NM) and Representative Richard Neal (D-MA) have introduced similar bills (S. 3760 in the Senate and HR 6099 in the House) to increase the proportion of workers who have access to a payroll deduction retirement savings account from the current 50 percent to almost 90 percent. Their bills,…

earliest_articles.title: Expanding Automatic Enrollment and Making Saver’s Credit a Match Will Help to Improve Retirement Security
earliest_articles.excerpt: Recent recommendations by the President’s Economic Recovery Advisory Board that would make the saver’s credit a match to individual savings by moderate income workers and further increase the use of automatic enrollment in retirement savings plans are the latest indication that policymakers recognize the need to…

newest_articles.title: State Auto-IRA Policies and Firm Behavior: Lessons from Administrative Tax Data

newest_articles.title: How Auto IRAs could soon improve retirement for millions of Americans
newest_articles.excerpt: On September 9, the House Ways and Means Committee took a potentially historic step that could help millions of Americans to build financial security. central_articles.title: Testimony before the Oregon Retirement Savings Task Force
central_articles.excerpt: Thank you for allowing me to testify before you today on the need to improve retirement savings opportunities for employees of private sector small businesses and ways to structure such an effort. central_articles.title: If we want universal savings, pay employers to sign us up for IRAs
central_articles.excerpt: My mother, long before she died at age 92, ran through both Social Security and her retirement money. She wasn’t unusual: the Employee Benefit Research Institute (EBRI) estimates that, even with Social Security, some 40+% of us will run out of money in retirement.[1] Why does this happen to so many? Often it’s because…
